scope.subject: Vol. 00 — Nest Runtime Specification Set — Introduction and Reading Map (00-introduction)

questions: What constitutes a knot in Nest runtime?
How does a knot relate to the wave-log virtual machine?
What is the significance of knots in implementing a conforming Nest runtime machine?
Can knots be understood independently of the reference implementation?

service.instruction: Integrate the understandings of knots in the Nest runtime by reconciling the conceptual, architectural, and implementational aspects, ensuring a cohesive view of knots as fundamental elements of the Nest runtime specification.

excerpts.section: Nest Runtime Specification Set — Introduction and Reading Map
excerpts.excerpt: This directory is the complete developer specification of the Nest runtime — the wave-log virtual machine. It is written in the manner of a processor architecture manual and is deliberately self-contained: everything needed to understand the machine, to implement a conforming machine in any programming language, and to write systems that run on it is inside this directory. TypeScript is used for demonstration only; every normative rule is stated in…

data.state: A knot in the Nest runtime refers to a concept described in Vol. 05 of the specification, which covers lifecycle, clews, deterministic and semantic winding, and budgets. Specifically, deterministic knots are detailed in Vol. 05 §4, and semantic knots are detailed in Vol. 05 §5.

data.reasoning: The definition of a knot in Nest runtime has been identified from the provided evidence, but the details of its implementation and interaction with other components, such as the wave log and membrane, are still unclear and require further integration of information from other volumes.

understanding.state: A knot in the Nest runtime refers to a concept described in Vol. 05 of the specification, which covers lifecycle, clews, deterministic and semantic winding, and budgets. Specifically, deterministic knots are detailed in Vol. 05 §4, and semantic knots are detailed in Vol. 05 §5.

excerpts.excerpt: This directory is the complete developer specification of the Nest runtime — the wave-log virtual machine. It is written in the manner of a processor architecture manual and is deliberately self-contained: everything needed to understand the machine, to implement a conforming machine in any programming language, and to write systems that run on it is inside this directory. TypeScript is used for demonstration only; every normative rule is stated in…

| Vol. | File | Contents | Status | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | 00 | [00-introduction.md](./00-introduction.md) | This map, conventions, naming | CURRENT | | 01 | [01-overview.md](./01-overview.md) | The machine at a glance: philosophy, figure, machine classes | CURRENT | | 02 | [02-machine-model.md](./02-machine-model.md) | Execution environment, stations, the architectural register file | CURRENT | | 03 | [03-wave-log.md](./03-wave-log.md) | The wave…

Naming note. In the reference project the bare word *Nest* historically named the workbench shell. This specification set names the whole machine the Nest runtime (equivalently, *the Nest virtual machine*, *Nest VM*), because the runtime is the nest in which waves live and the workbench is one shell over it. Where the workbench is meant, this set writes Nest Studio. An overloaded word is resolved by explicit definition, not by silent reuse — the same…

data.state: A knot, specifically an activation knot, relates to the wave-log virtual machine as it accumulates understanding and is a central entity in the Nest runtime, alongside the bind descriptor. The Nest runtime, also known as the Nest virtual machine or Nest VM, is a self-contained system that includes the wave-log virtual machine, with various components and volumes that describe its architecture, execution environment, and programming guide.

data.reasoning: The new material provides a clear introduction to the Nest runtime and its components, including the activation knot, but the relationship between the knot and the wave-log virtual machine is still somewhat indirect and requires further clarification from additional volumes, such as Vol. 05 on activation knots.

understanding.state: A knot, specifically an activation knot, relates to the wave-log virtual machine as it accumulates understanding and is a central entity in the Nest runtime, alongside the bind descriptor. The Nest runtime, also known as the Nest virtual machine or Nest VM, is a self-contained system that includes the wave-log virtual machine, with various components and volumes that describe its architecture, execution environment, and programming guide.

data.state: Knots, specifically activation knots, play a crucial role in implementing a conforming Nest runtime machine, as they accumulate understanding and are involved in the deterministic and semantic winding process, with lifecycle, clews, and budgets, as described in Volume 05 of the Nest Runtime Specification Set.

data.reasoning: The new material provides evidence of the importance of knots in the Nest runtime machine, but the understanding is still limited to the definition and role of activation knots, and more information is needed to fully address the significance of knots in implementing a conforming Nest runtime machine, such as their interaction with other components like bind descriptors and the membrane.

data.query: Vol. 00 — Nest Runtime Specification Set — Introduction and Reading Map (00-introduction) Can knots be understood independently of the reference implementation? specification self-containment

excerpts.section: Nest Runtime Specification Set — Introduction and Reading Map
excerpts.excerpt: The set was snapshotted from a working reference implementation (the Florispace Core project). That project's source is not required reading: the [refimpl book](./refimpl/00-book-map.md) inside this directory describes the reference implementation completely enough to build a fresh compatible one.

data.state: Knots can be understood independently of the reference implementation, as the specification set is self-contained and written in a language-neutral form, with every normative rule stated inside the directory, and the reference implementation book provides a complete description of one conforming implementation, allowing an engineer to build a fresh compatible implementation without consulting the project's source code.

data.reasoning: The addition of evidence from the Nest Runtime Specification Set and the Reference Implementation Book provides a clear indication that the specification is self-contained and language-neutral, allowing for independent understanding of knots, but the actual details of knots are still to be found in Vol. 05, which has not been provided.

understanding.state: Knots can be understood independently of the reference implementation, as the specification set is self-contained and written in a language-neutral form, with every normative rule stated inside the directory, and the reference implementation book provides a complete description of one conforming implementation, allowing an engineer to build a fresh compatible implementation without consulting the project's source code.
